prerequisites: Network access to the public Gutendex API at gutendex.com/books. No account, sign-up, or credentials are required.
Optionally decide which filters to apply: search terms, language codes, topic phrase, sort order, copyright status, or author birth-year range.

authMethods.name: No authentication
authMethods.summary: Gutendex is a free, open JSON REST API for Project Gutenberg catalog metadata and requires no API key, token, or credentials. The connector exposes no secret fields, and requests to gutendex.com/books are made anonymously.

setupSteps.title: Add the source (no credentials needed)
setupSteps.body: Create a new Gutendex source. Because the API is public and unauthenticated, there is nothing to enter for credentials. Requests are sent directly to gutendex.com/books over HTTPS.

setupSteps.title: Configure optional query filters
setupSteps.body: All configuration fields are optional and map to Gutendex query parameters. Set 'search' to match author names and book titles (space-separated, case-insensitive). Set 'languages' to a comma-separated list of two-character language codes (for example en or fr,fi). Set 'topic' to a case-insensitive phrase matched against bookshelves and subjects. Leave fields blank to return all books.

setupSteps.title: Set sorting, copyright, and author-year filters
setupSteps.body: Set 'sort' to 'popular' (the default, by download count), 'ascending', or 'descending' by Project Gutenberg ID. Set 'copyright' to 'true' (existing copyright), 'false' (US public domain), or 'null' (unknown); values can be combined with commas. Use 'author_year_start' and 'author_year_end' as positive (CE) or negative (BCE) integers to find books whose authors were alive within that range.

setupSteps.title: Verify the connection
setupSteps.body: Run a test sync. The connector queries gutendex.com/books and returns paginated JSON results (up to 32 book objects per page).
